sections.paragraph:
ὅστις, ἥτις, ὅ τι [ὅς and indefinite τις; ὅ τι is also written ὅ, τι and ὅτι]

—1. as a generalizing ref. to subject or obj. of a verb, anyone who, whoever, whatever Mt 5:39, 41; 10:32; Lk 10:35; 14:27; J 2:5; 1 Cor 16:2; Gal 5:10; Js 2:10.

—2. in specific ref. to an entity immediately preceding in the narrative

—a. apparently as simple ref. to a preceding entity = ὅς, ἥ, ὅν who, which Mk 15:7; Lk 2:4; 9:30; Ac 17:10; 23:14; 28:18; Eph 1:23.

—b. w. qualitative connotation namely one (those) who Mt 7:15, 26; 19:12 (‘such as’); Lk 2:10; Ac 10:41, 47; Ro 9:4 (οἵτινές εἰσιν inasmuch as they are); 2 Cor 8:10; 9:11; Eph 4:19; Phil 4:3; Hb 8:5; 1 Pt 2:11; Rv 2:24.

—c. unique formulation of ὅ. in an oblique case: ἕως ὅτου while Mt 5:25, but until Lk 15:8 v.l.
